goal: Tighten decision-request/v1 so it is emitted only for real human-in-the-loop authority boundaries, not routine uncertainty, and so stale-state or claim-support-related requests carry explicit boundary metadata.

forbiddenFiles: pr-closeout behavior changes except tests proving decision-request is not closeout proof
Linear, GitHub, CI, or external-service mutations
changes that make decision-request/v1 claim-supporting evidence

designConstraints: Decision requests remain governance_request_only and not_closeout_proof.
Routine uncertainty, preference questions, implementation-choice ambiguity, missing local context, and ordinary validation failures must not create decision debt.

Valid boundaries must be explicit and closed-taxonomy: destructive action, external mutation, credential or secret access, security-sensitive action, public contract change, release action, permission escalation, stale claim support, merge readiness, tracker authority, or goal completion.
Boundary metadata must be machine-readable so validators can distinguish true HILT authority from conversational uncertainty.

Stale-claim-support decision requests must never use freshness=current; stale-state or claim-support-related decision requests must include evidence refs and non-current staleState entries; a fresh no-evidence uncertainty request must fail closed.
Human-readable output must expose the boundary and blocker class textually, not only through JSON or color.
The slice must reuse the existing decision-request command and deep module instead of introducing a second decision workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: DecisionRequestPacket includes a hiltBoundary object with boundaryType, riskTier, reason, and blockerClass fields.
Public schema closes hiltBoundary.boundaryType, hiltBoundary.riskTier, and hiltBoundary.blockerClass with enums so non-CLI producers cannot submit unknown boundary strings.
Builder validation rejects routine uncertainty boundary types and emits a deterministic usage error.

Builder validation rejects stale_claim_support with freshness=current, and rejects stale_claim_support, merge_readiness, tracker_authority, and goal_completion boundaries without at least one evidence ref plus a non-current staleState entry.

Decision requests for destructive, external mutation, credential, security-sensitive, public contract, release, permission escalation, stale claim-support, merge-readiness, tracker-authority, and goal-completion boundaries pass with machine-readable boundary metadata.
CLI supports a bounded --boundary flag and prints boundary and blocker class in human output.
Public schema and example represent the new hiltBoundary fields and remain accepted by the runtime packet schema validator.
Routine uncertainty does not produce an open decision-request packet, so agents cannot hide ordinary ambiguity as human debt.
No delivery-truth, pr-closeout, runtime-card, or harness next behavior becomes claim-supporting because of this slice.
validationPlan: pnpm exec tsx src/cli.ts pr-closeout --repo . --pr 309 --json -> pass with current claim/evidence report or blocked with explicit missing external evidence

node --import tsx src/cli.ts decision-request --json --generated-at 2026-05-28T09:10:00.000Z --intent 'Choose whether to refresh stale external state before a merge-readiness claim.' --boundary merge_readiness --freshness stale --default-option refresh_external_state --option refresh_external_state='Refresh PR, CI, review, and tracker snapshots.' --evidence external-state:pr-309 --expires-at 2026-05-28T10:10:00.000Z --escalation-channel codex_thread -> pass

node --import tsx src/cli.ts decision-request --json --intent 'I am unsure which helper name to use.' --boundary routine_uncertainty --default-option ask --option ask='Ask the user.' -> fail with exit code 2 and error.code decision-request.invalid_boundary

pnpm exec biome check .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-032-spg-006-hilt-boundary-intent.json src/lib/decision-request src/commands/decision-request.test.ts contracts/decision-request.schema.json contracts/examples/decision-request.example.json contracts/runtime-packet-schemas.manifest.json src/dev/validate-runtime-packet-schemas-script.test.ts docs/cli-reference.md -> pass

git diff --check -- .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-032-spg-006-hilt-boundary-intent.json src/lib/decision-request src/commands/decision-request.test.ts contracts/decision-request.schema.json contracts/examples/decision-request.example.json contracts/runtime-packet-schemas.manifest.json src/dev/validate-runtime-packet-schemas-script.test.ts docs/cli-reference.md docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml artifacts/reviews/pu-032-spg-006-hilt-boundary-*.md
rollbackPlan: Remove hiltBoundary fields from decision-request types, builder, CLI, schema, and example.
Remove SPG-006-specific tests while preserving the existing GAP-005 decision-request governance command.
Preserve the existing governance_request_only and not_closeout_proof behavior.
stopConditions: Stop if implementation requires decision-request to become closeout proof or delivery-truth evidence.
Stop if routine uncertainty cannot be rejected without adding a broad prompt-classifier or AI judgment dependency.
Stop if the change would require live external mutation, secret access, or Codex repository writes.
